feedback.text: \(\log_{10}32=5\log_{10}2=1.5050\), not 3.2; 3.2 is neither a log value nor related here.

questiontext.text: The harmonic mean of 4 and 12 is

feedback.text: 8 is the arithmetic mean \((4+12)/2\); the harmonic mean is \(2/(1/4+1/12)=6\).
\(4\sqrt{3}\approx6.93\) is the geometric mean \(\sqrt{4\times12}\); the harmonic mean is 6.

questiontext.text: How many distinct 4-letter words (not necessarily meaningful) can be formed using the letters of the word EXAM if repetition is not allowed?

feedback.text: Correct. EXAM has 4 distinct letters (E, X, A, M). Number of 4-letter arrangements = \(4! = 24\).
12 would be the count if some letters repeated; EXAM has 4 distinct letters, giving \(4!=24\) arrangements.
256 \(= 4^4\) would be the count with repetition allowed; without repetition, it is \(4!=24\).
6 \(= 3!\) arrangements uses only 3 letters; using all 4 letters gives \(4!=24\).

questiontext.text: If 1st January 2024 is a Monday, what day of the week will 1st January 2025 be?

feedback.text: Correct. 2024 is a leap year (366 days). \(366 = 52\times7 + 2\). So 1 Jan 2025 is Monday + 2 = Wednesday.
Tuesday would be the case if 2024 were a non-leap year (365 = 52×7+1, advance by 1 day). Since 2024 is a leap year, advance is 2 days → Wednesday.
2024 is a leap year (366 days = 52 weeks + 2 days), so the day advances by 2: Monday → Wednesday, not Thursday.
The day would repeat only if 366 were divisible by 7, which it is not; it advances by 2 days to Wednesday.

questiontext.text: If \(3x + 2y = 18\) and \(x - y = 1\), what is \(x + y\)?

feedback.text: Correct. From \(x - y = 1\): \(x = y+1\). Substituting: \(3(y+1)+2y=18 \Rightarrow 5y+3=18 \Rightarrow y=3\), \(x=4\). So \(x+y=7\).
\(x=4, y=3\) gives \(x+y=7\); 9 would require e.g. \(x=5, y=4\), but then \(3(5)+2(4)=23\neq18\).
3 is the value of \(y\) alone; \(x+y=4+3=7\).

questiontext.text: A connected graph with \(n\) vertices and \(n-1\) edges (and no cycles) is called a

feedback.text: Correct. A tree is a connected acyclic graph. For a tree with \(n\) vertices, there are exactly \(n-1\) edges. This is a fundamental characterisation.
The complete graph \(K_n\) has \(n(n-1)/2\) edges (every pair connected); a tree has only \(n-1\) edges.
A bipartite graph has vertices split into two sets with edges only between sets; the number of edges is not restricted to \(n-1\).
An Eulerian graph has an Eulerian circuit (every vertex even degree); a tree (leaves have degree 1) is generally not Eulerian.
Correct. A barometer measures atmospheric pressure; a seismograph measures and records ground motion caused by earthquakes (seismic waves).
Temperature is measured by a thermometer; a seismograph records seismic (earthquake) activity.
Wind speed is measured by an anemometer; the seismograph records ground motion from earthquakes.
Humidity is measured by a hygrometer; a seismograph is specifically for earthquake detection.

questiontext.text: A product has successive discounts of 20% and 10%. What is the equivalent single discount?

feedback.text: Correct. After 20%: price = \(0.8P\). After 10% on that: \(0.9\times0.8P = 0.72P\). Single equivalent discount = \(1-0.72 = 28\%\).
30% would be the simple addition of the two discounts; the actual equivalent is \(100(1-0.8\times0.9)=28\%\).
Successive discounts of 20% and 10%: net multiplier = \(0.8\times0.9=0.72\), equivalent discount = 28%, not 25%.
18% = 20% − 10% (difference); the equivalent single discount is 28%, not the difference of the two discounts.

questiontext.text: In the cryptarithmetic puzzle SEND + MORE = MONEY, how many distinct digits appear in the solution?

answer.text: 8 distinct digits (S=9, E=5, N=6, D=7, M=1, O=0, R=8, Y=2)

feedback.text: Correct. The classic solution is S=9, E=5, N=6, D=7, M=1, O=0, R=8, Y=2. Verify: 9567+1085=10652. All 8 letters represent different digits.
SEND+MORE=MONEY uses 8 distinct letters; the digits 3 and 4 are unused in the standard solution.
There are 8 distinct letters (S,E,N,D,M,O,R,Y), each representing a unique digit, giving 8 distinct digits.

answer.text: 9 distinct digits (only one digit 0–9 is unused)

feedback.text: The standard solution uses digits 0,1,2,5,6,7,8,9 — that is 8 digits; two digits (3 and 4) are unused.

questiontext.text: For the data set: 4, 7, 3, 9, 7, 5, 7, 2, 6, the mode, median, and mean are respectively

feedback.text: Correct. Sorted: 2,3,4,5,6,7,7,7,9 — Mode = 7 (appears 3 times); Median = 5th of 9 = 6; Sum = 50, Mean = 50/9 ≈ 5.56.
The median is the 5th value in the sorted list 2,3,4,5,6,7,7,7,9, which is 6, not 7.
The mean is 50/9 ≈ 5.56, not 7; the median is 6 (5th of 9 sorted values).
Mode is 7 (appearing 3 times), not 5; the median (sorted 5th value) is 6 and the mean is 50/9.

questiontext.text: A number is first increased by 25% and then decreased by 20%. What is the net percentage change?

feedback.text: Correct. Net multiplier: \(1.25\times0.80 = 1.00\). The number returns to its original value — 0% net change.
\(1.25\times0.80=1.00\); there is no net increase or decrease.
Net multiplier = \(1.25\times0.80=1.00\), meaning no net change; this is a well-known result (25% up, 20% down cancel exactly).
\(1.25\times0.80=1.00\), giving 0% net change, not 2% increase.

questiontext.text: A is the father of B. B is the sister of C. C is the mother of D. How is A related to D?

feedback.text: Correct. A is B's father. B is C's sister. So A is also C's father. C is D's mother. Therefore A is D's maternal grandfather.
A is C's father (since B is C's sister and A is B's father), making A D's grandfather, not uncle.
A is the father of C (D's mother), making him the maternal grandfather, not the paternal grandfather (which would be the father of D's father).
A is D's grandfather (two generations removed), not D's father.

questiontext.text: Which of the following is the largest?

feedback.text: Correct. Compare by raising to the 6th power: \((\sqrt{2})^6=8\), \((\sqrt[3]{3})^6=9\), \((\sqrt[3]{5})^6=25\), \((\sqrt[6]{10})^6=10\). Largest is \(\sqrt[3]{5}\) (giving 25).
To the 6th power: \((\sqrt2)^6=8 < 25=(\sqrt[3]5)^6\).
Correct. An acrobat uses a trapeze as their primary instrument/apparatus; an archer uses a bow as their primary instrument.

An arrow is the projectile fired, but the bow is the primary instrument of an archer — analogous to the trapeze as an apparatus. (Though "arrow" is debatable, "bow" is the standard equipment analogy.)
A target is what the archer aims at, not their instrument; the trapeze is the acrobat's instrument, so the archer's instrument (bow) is the correct analogy.
A quiver holds arrows but is not the archer's primary instrument in the way a trapeze is used by an acrobat; the bow is the correct analogy.

questiontext.text: A farmer has 120 m of fencing to enclose a rectangular field adjacent to a river (no fence needed along the river). What dimensions maximize the enclosed area?

answer.text: Width = 30 m (two sides), Length = 60 m (parallel to river); Area = 1800 m²

feedback.text: Correct. With two widths and one length: \(2w+l=120\Rightarrow l=120-2w\). Area \(A=wl=w(120-2w)=120w-2w^2\). Maximum at \(w=30\): \(l=60\), \(A=1800\) m².

answer.text: Width = 40 m, Length = 40 m; Area = 1600 m²
Width = 20 m, Length = 80 m; Area = 1600 m²
Width = 60 m, Length = 0 m; Area = 0 m²

feedback.text: A degenerate case with zero length gives zero area; the maximum is at \(w=30\), \(l=60\), \(A=1800\) m².

questiontext.text: If the alphabet is written in reverse (Z=1, Y=2, X=3, …, A=26), what is the sum of the values of the letters in the word CAT?

feedback.text: C=24, A=26, T=7 in the reverse alphabet; their sum is 57, not 60.
24 is the value of C alone; C+A+T = 24+26+7 = 57.

answer.text:
45]])</text> <feedback><text>45 does not correspond to the sum of C+A+T in any standard reverse alphabet coding.</text></feedback> </answer> </question>
<!-- Q17 --> <question type="multichoice"> <name><text>PARTA5_Q17_PureLogic_Truth</text></name> <questiontext format="html"> <text><![CDATA[Which of the following compound propositions is a tautology (always true)?

feedback.text: Correct. \(P\vee\neg P\) is true regardless of the truth value of \(P\) — if \(P\) is true, the disjunction is true; if \(P\) is false, \(\neg P\) is true, making the disjunction true. It is a tautology.
\(P\wedge\neg P\) is always false (a contradiction), the opposite of a tautology.
\(P\Rightarrow Q\) is false when \(P\) is true and \(Q\) is false; it is not a tautology.
\(P\wedge Q\) is false whenever \(P\) or \(Q\) is false; it is not a tautology.

questiontext.text: What comes next in the series: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?

feedback.text: Correct. First letters go forward: A, B, C, D, E. Second letters go backward: Z, Y, X, W, V. Next pair: EV.
The second letter is W in DW, so next is V (not U); the pair is EV.
The first letter advances from D to E (not F); the next pair is EV.
W is the second letter of DW; for the next term, W retreats to V, giving EV not EW.

questiontext.text: Which of the following is an example of a scalar quantity in physics?

feedback.text: Correct. Temperature has only magnitude (no direction), making it a scalar. Vector quantities (e.g., force, velocity, acceleration) have both magnitude and direction.
Velocity is a vector (magnitude = speed + direction); temperature, mass, and energy are scalars.
Force is a vector quantity (has direction); temperature is a scalar.
Displacement is a vector (magnitude + direction); distance (scalar) is its magnitude.
47 in octal is not the same as 47 in decimal; \((47)_8 = 4\times8+7 = 39_{10}\).
